Description: As a VP, I see headline KPIs in Briefing mode so I can get a 10-second status read. As an analyst, I toggle to Explore mode for full interactive filters. AC: Each of 6 tabs has Briefing and Explore mode. Briefing shows 3-5 KPI cards with trend arrows. Explore unlocks all filters and chart interactions. Default mode is Briefing. Toggle persists within session.

As an analyst, I can pin notes to specific data points or time ranges so the dashboard becomes institutional memory. AC: Click any chart point to add annotation. Annotations persist across sessions (localStorage or DB). Annotations show author and timestamp. Filter annotations by date range.

As a user, I receive anomaly notifications that deep-link to the relevant tab with time range pre-filtered so I find issues without hunting. AC: Anomaly detection triggers notification. Notification contains deep-link URL. Deep-link opens correct tab with correct time range. Works for all 6 tabs.

As a user, I see data vintage on each widget so I know how current the information is. AC: Each widget header shows 'Updated X min ago'. Freshness badge turns amber when approaching staleness threshold. Turns red when stale (per E2 thresholds). Updates automatically.

As a user, I see shaded uncertainty cones that widen as forecast horizon extends so I understand precision without statistical literacy. AC: Tab 1 forecast chart shows 80% and 95% confidence bands. Bands widen monotonically into the future. Legend explains confidence levels. Works for Prophet model (primary).

Design principle: never show a number alone. Every metric has automatic comparator. AC: All KPI cards show value + delta vs comparator. Comparators: vs yesterday, vs same day last year, vs forecast. Format: '4,200 MW (↑6% vs expected)'. Applied to all 6 tabs. Trend arrows (↑↓→) included.

As a user, the dashboard opens with an auto-generated natural-language summary so I get context before diving into charts. AC: Summary generated from threshold logic (not LLM). Covers: demand forecast vs baseline, weather driver, generation capacity, active alerts. Updates on region change. Persona-specific language.

As a returning user, I see a session-aware diff so I know what changed since my last visit. AC: Tracks last visit timestamp in localStorage. Shows 3-5 most significant changes. Changes: forecast revisions, new alerts, model updates, data source changes. Dismissible.

As a user, the dashboard remembers my last 3 filter states so I can pick up where I left off. AC: localStorage stores last region, time range, active tab per user. 'Resume last session' option on load. Not a full personalization engine.

As a user, charts animate smoothly when switching time ranges so I maintain spatial context. AC: Plotly animate=True on time range change. Transition duration 300-500ms. No animation on tab switch (instant). Works on all chart types in all tabs.

As a user viewing a pricing spike, the dashboard surfaces related anomalies on other tabs so I can correlate events. AC: Event correlation on shared time axis. Links navigate to correct tab with same time range. Works between Tab 1↔5, Tab 4↔5, Tab 5↔6.

As a user, I can save filter + time range + tab combinations as named shareable URLs. AC: Save button creates named bookmark. Bookmark serializes: region, tab, time range, persona, scenario params. Shareable URL (query params). Load bookmark restores exact state.

Summary: [C3] Inline Why Tooltips on Model Outputs
Description: As a user, I hover on a forecast spike and see top 3 contributing factors with weights. AC: Hover tooltip shows top 3 SHAP features + weights. Format: 'temperature +4°F (42%), weekday (31%), industrial (27%)'. Works on Tab 1 forecast chart and Tab 6 scenario chart. Powered by XGBoost SHAP values.

As a user, I drag a playhead timeline to scrub through time and all widgets animate together. AC: Playhead component at bottom of tab. All charts sync to playhead timestamp. Play/pause button for auto-advance. Speed control (1x, 2x, 4x).

As a power user, I press Cmd+K to search and jump to any tab/region/scenario. AC: Cmd+K (Mac) or Ctrl+K (Win) opens search modal. Searches: tab names, region names, saved scenarios. Selection navigates to result. Fuzzy matching.

As a stakeholder, I record actions taken based on dashboard data for regulatory compliance. AC: Sidebar panel with text input. Log entry captures: timestamp, user, action, data state reference. Entries are immutable (append-only). Exportable to CSV. FERC/NERC defensible.

As a user, information hierarchy adapts to operational context (hurricane season, rate case period). AC: Mode selector: Normal, Hurricane Season, Rate Case, Peak Season. Each mode promotes relevant widgets. Can be triggered by calendar or manual selection.

As a user, I export dashboard views in 3 formats: briefing slide, analyst CSV, clipboard image. AC: Export button with 3 options. 'Briefing slide' = auto-formatted with narrative title. 'Analyst dataset' = full CSV with all visible data. 'Copy chart' = PNG to clipboard.

As a user presenting in a meeting, one button strips navigation chrome and reformats for projection. AC: Toggle button in header. Hides: sidebar, filters, navigation tabs (shows active tab only). Expands charts to full width. Narrative becomes slide title. Annotations visible.

As an admin, data visibility is scoped by user role and territory. AC: Define security model: which roles see which BAs. Implement at data query layer (not just UI hiding). Default: all BAs visible for portfolio demo. Extensible for multi-team deployment.

As a compliance officer, I can trace which model version, weather data vintage, and features produced each forecast. AC: Log per forecast: model version, training data date range, weather data timestamp, feature list. Queryable by date range. Retained for 1 year. FERC/NERC defensible.

As an operator, API keys rotate on schedule and rate limits are handled gracefully. AC: EIA key stored in Secret Manager. Rotation cadence documented. Rate limit hit → exponential backoff → serve stale cache. NOAA rate limits respected. Alert on repeated rate limit hits.

As an engineer, every data source has defined schema, value ranges, and null handling. AC: EIA: demand_mw > 0, < 500000. Weather: temp -50 to 150°F. Wind: 0-200 mph. Null handling: interpolate < 6h gap, flag > 6h. Schema validation on every API response.

As a user, I know when data is too old to trust. AC: Weather: 2h. Generation: 5min. Pricing: 15min. Demand: 1h. Alerts: 30min. Threshold exceeded → amber badge. Double threshold → red badge. Defined in config.py.
As a user, I see green/amber/red reliability badges per widget. AC: Builds on A4 + E2. Green: fresh. Amber: approaching stale. Red: stale. Badge on every widget header. Tooltip explains threshold.
As a user, every tab loads in < 2 seconds (p95). AC: Performance budget defined per tab. Measured in CI (lighthouse or custom). Regression = blocked PR.

As an engineer, caching rules are explicit per data type. AC: Historical data: aggressive (TTL = 24h). Near-real-time: short TTL (15min). Forecasts: cache until model refresh (24h). Cache invalidation on manual refresh. Documented in config.py.
As an engineer, large time range queries use pre-computed rollups. AC: Daily/weekly/monthly aggregations computed on data refresh. On-demand only for < 7 day ranges. Rollup tables in SQLite cache.

As a user, when one data source is down, only affected widgets degrade — not the whole dashboard. AC: Per-widget fallback defined: last-known-good with timestamp, grayed-out, partial view. Specified per tab in spec. No full-page error screens.

As a user, when NOAA/EIA/Open-Meteo is down, I see stale data with a warning — not an error page. AC: Per-source fallback: stale cache + warning badge. If no cache exists: informative empty state. Logged with structlog. Alert if down > 1 hour.
As a PM, every feature has testable acceptance criteria. AC: Audit all features against spec AC-1 through AC-7. Gap analysis documented. Missing criteria added before sprint commitment.

As a data scientist, model accuracy thresholds are defined for governance. AC: MAPE targets: excellent < 3%, target < 5%, acceptable < 10%, rollback > 15%. Per forecast horizon (24h, 48h, 72h, 7d). Automated check in CI. Defined in config.py.

As an engineer, the test strategy is explicit. AC: Unit: data/ ≥90%, models/ ≥85%, simulation/ ≥90%, personas/ ≥95%. Integration: 4 pipeline tests. E2E: 3 dashboard tests. Coverage enforced in CI. Documented in CLAUDE.md.

As an engineer debugging a wrong number, I can trace through every ETL step. AC: structlog at each transformation: raw → cleaned → aligned → feature-engineered → model-ready. Log record counts, null counts, value ranges at each step.

As an operator, I'm alerted when the data pipeline is unhealthy. AC: Health = all 3 APIs responding + cache fresh + models trained within 24h. Health endpoint: GET /health returns JSON status. Alert if unhealthy > 15 min.

As an engineer, I know which values differ between dev/staging/prod. AC: Matrix in config.py: env-specific values (cache TTL, log level, min instances). All configurable via env vars. Documented in .env.example. No hardcoded environment-specific values.

As an engineer, I can toggle features on/off per environment. AC: Simple dict in config.py: {feature: bool}. Checked at render time. No external service. Flags: each tab, persona switcher, scenario presets.

As an engineer, every external API has a defined contract. AC: Per API: expected schema, response format, error codes, retry policy, rate limits, versioning. How to detect upstream format changes. Documented in docs/.

As an engineer, internal module boundaries have defined interfaces. AC: data/ → models/: DataFrame schema. models/ → components/: forecast array shape. simulation/ → components/: scenario result schema. Type hints enforce.

As a user with accessibility needs, the dashboard meets WCAG 2.1 AA standards. AC: Color-independent indicators (not just red/green). Screen reader labels on data tables. Keyboard navigation for all interactive elements. Contrast ratio ≥ 4.5:1.
As a compliance officer, data presentation meets FERC/NERC requirements. AC: Data retention periods defined. Audit trail (D2) meets regulatory needs. Data export meets reporting format requirements.
As an operator, I have a documented release and rollback process. AC: Blue-green deployment via Cloud Run revisions. Rollback command documented. Model version rollback procedure. Release checklist.

As an operator, I understand compute/storage costs at current and projected scale. AC: Cost estimate at current scale (< $5/month). Cost projection at 10x, 100x traffic. Cost review triggers defined. GCP billing alerts set.
As a PM, buildplan and expanded spec stay in sync. AC: Expanded spec = source of truth. Sync check at every Sprint Review. Update rule: if implementation deviates, spec updated same sprint.
As a new user, I learn to use the dashboard quickly. AC: In-app guidance or README walkthrough. Covers: persona selection, tab navigation, scenario simulator, export. < 5 min to first useful insight.
As a user, I'm notified of dashboard changes. AC: Release notes in README. In-app banner for major changes. 'What's new' dismissible modal. Prevents 'who moved my chart' confusion.
